questions.question: What is the main difference between backward chaining and forward chaining?
expectedPoints: Backward chaining is goal-driven (starts with hypothesis)
Forward chaining is data-driven (starts with facts)
Backward chaining is more efficient for specific queries
Forward chaining generates all possible conclusions

questions.question: Explain backward chaining with a concrete example using first-order logic rules and facts. Include the unification process in your explanation.
expectedPoints: Example knowledge base with rules and facts
Step-by-step tracing of the backward chaining process
Explanation of unification with variable binding
